Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: High strength Al–Li alloy development for laser powder bed fusion

Composition_Info.Note: Powder composition: Al-3.89Cu-1.22Li-0.98Sc-0.43Zr; LPBF sample composition: Al-3.93Cu-1.11Li-0.97Sc-0.42Zr

Process_Text.original: High purity Al (99.99%), Al-10Li, Al-50Cu, Al-2Sc-1Zr master alloys used for ingot preparation. Ingots sectioned to 30mm dia x 5mm thick cylinders, polished to P800 grit. Laser glazing performed on in-house LPBF machine with IPG YLR-500 fiber laser (max 500W). Optimized composition converted to powder by inert gas atomization (Hunan Hengji Powder Technology). LPBF processing on LiM-X260A machine: laser power 300W, scanning velocity 1200 mm/s, hatch spacing 0.1 mm, layer thickness 40 um, hatch rotation 90 deg. Cuboid samples 8x8x6 mm, tensile plates 84x5x11 mm per ASTM B557M-10.
Process_Text.simplified: Inert gas atomized powder processed by LPBF (LiM-X260A); optimized LPBF params: 300W, 1200mm/s, 0.1mm hatch, 40um layer, 99.9% density

Process_Info.Equipment: LiM-X260A LPBF machine; IPG YLR-500 fiber laser (in-house machine)

Microstructure_Text.original: Bimodal grain microstructure consisting of fine equiaxed grains and fine columnar grains. Equiaxed grains mainly located at molten pool boundaries and columnar grains located inside molten pools. The equiaxed region was caused by the high number density of primary Al3(Sc,Zr) nucleation sites. The columnar region was related to the low number density of nucleation sites associated with the high temperature gradient. Average equivalent circle diameters: equiaxed grains 0.6 um, columnar grains 2.5 um. Much finer than crack-free 2195 samples (37.9 um avg).
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Bimodal microstructure: fine equiaxed grains (0.6 um) at molten pool boundaries + fine columnar grains (2.5 um) inside pools; Al3(Sc,Zr) nucleation sites refine grains

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; quasistatic testing with 25 mm clip gauge extensometer
at room temperature; quasistatic testing with 25 mm clip gauge extensometer
at room temperature; quasistatic testing with 25 mm clip gauge extensometer

Composition_Info.Note: Same nominal composition as LPBF specimen, produced by conventional casting

Process_Text.original: High purity Al (99.99%), Al-10Li, Al-50Cu, Al-2Sc-1Zr (wt%) master alloy used for casting. Ingots prepared by conventional casting method.
Process_Text.simplified: Conventional casting using high purity Al and master alloys

Microstructure_Text.original: Cast microstructure with large amount of coarse Al3(Sc,Zr) particles and possible metallurgical defects. Grain size: average equivalent circle diameter 18.2 um (from cast region of sample #5). Inoculation with Sc and Zr refined grains from 257.9 um (sample #1) to finer sizes.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Coarse Al3(Sc,Zr) particles and coarse grain structure; much inferior properties compared to LPBF counterpart
